commit.message:
Add react-native as devDependency to ts-http-runtime (#38027)

## What

Add `react-native` as a devDependency to `@typespec/ts-http-runtime`,
replacing the hand-written type shim with real package types, and give
`concat-react-native.mts` its own polyfill-friendly implementation.

## Why

### Hermes implements a subset of web APIs — its types diverge from DOM

React Native uses the [Hermes](https://hermesengine.dev/) JavaScript
engine, which provides its own implementations of web APIs (`Blob`,
`FormData`, `TextDecoder`, etc.) rather than deferring to a full browser
engine. Hermes does **not** implement the complete DOM/Web API surface —
its built-in types are a **subset** of what `lib.dom.d.ts` declares:

- **`Blob` constructor** — accepts only `(Blob | string)[]`, not the
full `BlobPart[]` union (`ArrayBufferView` / `ArrayBuffer` / `string`).
Passing a `Uint8Array` throws at runtime. See
[facebook/react-native#44125](https://github.com/facebook/react-native/issues/44125).
Thanks @jeremymeng for the investigation.
- **`Uint8Array` → `Blob`** — works as a data type, but cannot be passed
to the Blob constructor without a polyfill. See
[facebook/react-native#41079](https://github.com/facebook/react-native/issues/41079).
Thanks @jeremymeng for the investigation.
- **`ReadableStream` / `Response`** — not available by default. Apps can
polyfill these.
- **`Platform` API** — React Native-specific, no DOM equivalent.
- **`globals.d.ts`** — react-native ships its own global type
declarations that overlap with but are narrower than `lib.dom.d.ts`,
causing type conflicts when both are loaded.

This means our hand-written `react-native.d.ts` shim (which only
declared `Platform`) was incomplete, and we were missing type errors
that surface only under the real react-native types.

## Changes

- **`pnpm-workspace.yaml`** — added `react-native: ^0.84.1` and
`@types/react: ^19.2.14` to the `testing` catalog
- **`sdk/core/ts-http-runtime/package.json`** — added `react-native:
catalog:testing` and `@types/react: catalog:testing` to devDependencies
- **`src/util/react-native.d.ts`** — deleted (replaced by real package
types)
- **`src/util/concat-react-native.mts`** — standalone RN implementation
that handles all source types (`Blob`, `Uint8Array`, `ReadableStream`)
and defers to the runtime. Apps with appropriate polyfills get correct
behavior; apps without them get the engine's native error. Uses a type
assertion (`as (Blob | string)[]`) to satisfy RN's stricter `BlobPart`
types at compile time. Includes detailed JSDoc documenting Hermes
limitations with links to upstream issues. Thanks @timovv for the great
discussion.
- **`src/util/concat.common.ts` → `src/util/concat-browser.mts`** —
since `concat-react-native.mts` is now standalone, `concat.common.ts`
was only used by the browser target. Inlined its content directly into
`concat-browser.mts` and deleted the now-unused common file.
- **`tsconfig.browser.config.json`** — added `skipLibCheck: true` to
avoid type conflicts between react-native's `globals.d.ts` (Hermes
subset types) and `lib.dom.d.ts` (full browser types)
- **`pnpm-lock.yaml`** — updated with react-native and transitive
dependencies

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Deyaa Eldeen <deyaaeldeen@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: wire up file picker change handler for attach button (#558)

## Problem

The attach image button (📎) does nothing on mobile. Tapping it opens the
native file picker, but selecting an image has no effect — the image is
silently dropped.

## Root Cause

The button clicks a hidden `<input type="file">` via `clickElement()` JS
interop, which correctly opens the native picker. But **there is no
`change` event listener** on the file input, so when the user selects a
file, nothing reads it.

On desktop, image attachment works via **paste** and **drag-drop**,
which have their own global JS listeners. The file picker button has
never actually worked on any platform — it's just more noticeable on
mobile where paste/drag-drop aren't available.

## Fix

Add a global delegated `change` event listener for `<input type="file">`
elements matching the `file-{sessionName}` pattern. When files are
selected:
1. Read each image file via `FileReader`
2. Call `JsImagePasted` (the same Blazor interop used by paste and
drag-drop)
3. Reset the input value so the same file can be re-selected

This follows the exact same pattern as the existing paste and drop
handlers.

## Testing
- ✅ All 3319 tests pass
- ✅ Mac Catalyst build succeeds
- Needs manual verification on Android/iOS that the native file picker →
image attachment flow works end-to-end

commit.message:
fix: make benchmark k6 results writable, increase Keycloak resources, and fix e2e-kind race condition (#56)

* fix: make benchmark results dir writable for k6 and increase Keycloak resources

The grafana/k6 Docker image runs as non-root uid 12345, which could
not write to the bind-mounted results/ directory. This caused K6_OUT
initialization to fail, aborting all benchmark tests (0 HTTP requests).

Additionally:
- Increase Keycloak memory from 512M to 1024M (JVM needs more in CI)
- Increase Keycloak health wait from 180s to 300s for CI runners

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/ianlintner/rust-oauth2-server/sessions/3d751478-eb97-400b-a953-039d8efd0900

Co-authored-by: ianlintner <500914+ianlintner@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: use --user flag for k6 instead of chmod 777

Run k6 as the host user via --user "$(id -u):$(id -g)" so it can
write to the bind-mounted results directory. This is more targeted
than making the directory world-writable.

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/ianlintner/rust-oauth2-server/sessions/3d751478-eb97-400b-a953-039d8efd0900

Co-authored-by: ianlintner <500914+ianlintner@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: resolve e2e-kind race condition where server starts before migrations

The wait-for-migrations init container used `|| true` which allowed the
oauth2-server to start before Flyway completed database migrations. This
caused the seed user creation to fail (users table didn't exist yet) and
the admin login smoke test to get invalid_credentials.

Fixes:
- Remove `|| true` from init container so it properly blocks until
  migrations complete
- Add rollout restart in e2e script after Flyway finishes to guarantee
  pods start with a fully-migrated database

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/ianlintner/rust-oauth2-server/sessions/37cdb3f9-3cdb-4859-9ca5-99f677a5c435

Co-authored-by: ianlintner <500914+ianlintner@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: add descriptive error message to migration init container on failure

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/ianlintner/rust-oauth2-server/sessions/37cdb3f9-3cdb-4859-9ca5-99f677a5c435

Co-authored-by: ianlintner <500914+ianlintner@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: add batch/jobs RBAC permissions for wait-for-migrations init container

The wait-for-migrations init container uses `kubectl wait --for=condition=complete job/flyway-migration`
which requires get/list/watch permissions on batch/jobs. The oauth2-server
Role only had permissions for secrets and configmaps, causing the init
container to fail with a permission error and enter CrashLoopBackOff.

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/ianlintner/rust-oauth2-server/sessions/2e1a7481-230d-4565-bd0e-62f32d5387b1

commit.message:
fix: ClassifyByKeywords recognises CI/CD patterns (ci/ prefix) (#147)

'CI/CD pipeline completed' was not classified as build because
ClassifyByKeywords only checked for 'ci ' (trailing space), missing
the 'CI/CD' style where a slash follows 'CI'.

Adding the 'ci/' check catches:
  - 'CI/CD pipeline completed'
  - 'CI/CD workflow triggered'
  - Any pattern where 'CI' is immediately followed by a slash

False-positive analysis: common English words containing 'ci' (recipe,
social, special, facial...) are followed by letters, not '/', so the
new check introduces no false matches.

Regression tests: two new InlineData entries in KeywordFallback_BackwardCompatible.

commit.message:
perf: replace block_in_place+block_on with sync cache in consensus

Add parking_lot::RwLock-backed cached_active / cached_all snapshot fields
to MasternodeRegistry. The caches are rebuilt inside each masternodes write
lock scope (register_internal, deregister, unregister, cleanup_stale_reports,
apply_masternode_reg squatter eviction, apply_collateral_unlock), guaranteeing
the snapshots are always consistent with the underlying HashMap.

Replace all 7 block_in_place(|| block_on(registry.list_active/all())) calls
in consensus.rs (get_validators, accumulate_timevote, verify_timeproof,
check_prepare_consensus, check_precommit_consensus, get_masternodes,
get_masternodes_with_rewards) with direct active_masternodes_cached() /
all_masternodes_cached() calls.

Impact:
- Eliminates tokio OS-thread spawning under concurrent vote load (block_in_place
  converts a worker thread to a blocking thread; under 4+ concurrent callers
  tokio had to spawn extra OS threads, growing unboundedly during flood attacks)
- Consensus functions now run in ~100 ns (parking_lot read + Vec clone) instead
  of crossing the async/blocking boundary
- RPC and networking tasks no longer starve while consensus holds worker threads
- Fixes runtime starvation on 1-core / 2-thread VPS hosts where block_in_place
  could freeze the entire tokio runtime

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: auto-reconnect port-forward in kubectl controller proxy (#16)

When the underlying port-forward dies (pod restart, network timeout),
the proxy now detects the connection failure and transparently
re-establishes the port-forward to a new controller pod. Requests
that trigger the reconnect are retried once after the new connection
is established.

The proxyBackend struct implements http.RoundTripper with:
- Connection health check via TCP dial before each request
- Automatic reconnect on connection refused or dial errors
- Pod re-discovery on reconnect (picks up new pods after restarts)
- Thread-safe backend port and transport management

- Add SBE008 (UnresolvedTypeReference) and SBE009 (InvalidNumericConstraint)
  diagnostics for precise error reporting during code generation
- Validate required schema attributes (name, id, type, primitiveType,
  encodingType) via GetRequiredAttribute, emitting SBE002 on missing
- Replace ArgumentException throws in GetTypeLength() with SBE008
  diagnostic and safe fallback, preventing full schema abort
- Report SBE001 when sinceVersion is non-empty but not a valid integer
- Validate minValue/maxValue as numeric before emitting validation code;
  report SBE009 and skip invalid constraints
- Isolate generator phases with individual try/catch blocks so one bad
  message/type does not prevent generation of remaining schema elements
- Report SBE005 when dimensionType composite is not found, falling back
  to ushort for numInGroup

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Replace bare dictionary indexers with safe TryGetValue accessors

Replace all bare TypesCatalog.PrimitiveTypeLengths[] and
TypesCatalog.NullValueByType[] indexer accesses with safe
GetPrimitiveLength() and GetNullValue() methods that use
TryGetValue and return sensible defaults instead of throwing
KeyNotFoundException for unknown primitive types.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Emit SBE010 warning when fallback values are used for unknown primitive types

Add SBE010 (UnknownPrimitiveTypeFallback) diagnostic that warns at
build time when a primitive type is not recognized in TypesCatalog
for length or null sentinel lookups. Previously, GetPrimitiveLength()
and GetNullValue() would silently return fallback values (0 and
'default'), potentially producing incorrect generated code.

Validation is performed in the generators (which have access to
SourceProductionContext) before or after creating definition objects:
- TypesCodeGenerator: enum primitive length, optional type null
  sentinel, composite field null sentinel
- MessagesCodeGenerator: optional message field null sentinel

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

  for SBE007-SBE010; updated purpose list and implementation notes
- CHANGELOG.md: [Unreleased] section with all security fixes, new
  diagnostics, safe accessors, and generator isolation

fix: prevent pull-to-refresh transform from breaking fixed-position modals

The PullToRefresh component always applied transform: translateY(0px) and
will-change: transform to .ptr-content, even when idle. A CSS transform on
any ancestor creates a new containing block for position: fixed descendants,
causing modals (Edit Device, Share, etc.) to be positioned relative to the
wrapper instead of the viewport.

Only apply transform and will-change when actively pulling (pullDistance > 0).

commit.message:
perf(maglev): fused mono-dispatch for closure calls

Switch mono-cache HIT path from 3-step (prepare + x86 call + finish)
to single fused dispatch function. This eliminates:
- 1 Cell<RtPtrs>::get() copy (56 bytes per call)
- 1 Rust function prologue/epilogue
- DirectCallState TLS roundtrip (kept on Rust stack instead)

Also skip register-file zeroing for closures with ≤2 slots
(callee overwrites before reading).

commit.message:
feat: support viewing workflow runs without PR context (#32)



## Summary

Adds support for viewing a workflow run directly, without needing a PR:

```bash
# Via URL
gh enhance https://github.com/owner/repo/actions/runs/23687980056

# Via run ID (--run disambiguates from PR numbers)
gh enhance 23687980056 --run
```

### Changes

**`cmd/enhance/root.go`** — URL parsing + CLI
- Detects `/actions/runs/{id}` in URLs alongside existing `/pull/{n}`
handling
- Adds `--run` flag for bare numeric IDs
- Updated usage string and examples

**`internal/data/data.go`** — Data model
- Added `DisplayTitle` field to `WorkflowRun` struct (used to show PR
title for PR-triggered runs instead of duplicating the workflow name)

**`internal/tui/tui.go`** — Model + views
- Added `runID` field and `RunID` to `ModelOpts`
- `Init()` branches to run mode when `runID` is set
- `viewRunModeHeader()`: shows run status pill, repo ⋅ run ID, and
display title
- Refactored footer: `appendStatTexts()` and `renderFooterLayout()` are
shared between PR and run mode footers, eliminating duplication
- `isRunModeInProgress()`: checks whether the run is still in progress
- Handles `runModeFetchedMsg` and `runModeIntervalTickMsg` in `Update()`

**`internal/tui/cmds.go`** — Data fetching
- `startSpinners()`: extracted shared spinner init (reused by both
`makeInitCmd` and `makeRunModeInitCmd`)
- `makeRunModeInitCmd()`: alternate init path using REST API
- `fetchRun()`: fetches run + jobs via
`FetchWorkflowRunByID`/`FetchWorkflowRunJobs`
- `fetchRunWithInterval()`: polls until run completes (with rate limit
guard)
- `convertRunResponseToWorkflowRun()`: maps REST responses to existing
`data.WorkflowRun`/`WorkflowJob` types

### What stays unchanged

The core TUI components — job list, step list, log viewer, rerun
actions, and all delegates — work without modification. Run mode maps
REST data into the same types they already consume.

### Testing

- `go build ./...` ✅
- `golangci-lint` ✅
- `go test ./...` ✅

Closes #19

Demo:


![run-mode-demo](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/1cc1d3d7-c819-4372-969e-2b96ff610f83)

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
r600: upgrade KCACHE from UNPROVEN to UNSAFE with ISA evidence

ISA §9.2 confirms KCACHE has NO buffer SIZE field — only 256-byte
cache-line boundaries.  Relative-indexed OOB returns NaN (0x7FFFFFFF),
static OOB returns undefined, within-line-past-size leaks cache contents.
None satisfy Vulkan robustness (zero return required).

Cross-document update (all 3 sources now aligned):
- ABI contract §5: KCACHE declared UNSAFE, ISA evidence cited
- ABI contract §10 Gap 1: upgraded from UNPROVEN to CONFIRMED UNSAFE
- ABI contract §10 Gap 3: added per-thread masking ISA confirmation
- ABI contract §11 Q1: probe now confirmatory, not blocking
- Blueprint robust_read_access: ISA §9.2 NaN behavior documented
- OOB findings table: KCACHE row changed from ⚠️ UNPROVEN to ❌ UNSAFE

Zero remaining UNPROVEN mentions across all documents.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
